name: Alhaurín de la Torre – circular route to Alhaurín el Grande
name_original: Alhaurín de la Torre – ruta circular hasta Alhaurín el Grande
region_intro: Road cycling around Alhaurín De La Torre offers diverse terrain, situated between the eastern slopes of the Sierra de Mijas mountain range and the fertile Guadalhorce Valley. The region features varied landscapes, from pine forests to lush vegetable gardens, with notable areas like Sierra Jabalcuza and Sierra Jarapalos. Cyclists encounter well-maintained roads and challenging climbs, providing a range of experiences from gentle rides to rigorous ascents. This geographical mix makes it a compelling destination for road cyclists seeking varied elevation and scenic routes. Best road cycling routes around Alhaurín De La Torre The most popular road cycling route is Alhaurín de la Torre – circular route to Alhaurín el Grande, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coín – Alozaina Arch loop from Campanillas, a difficult 60.9 miles (98.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Playa de Fuengirola – Elephant Roundabout loop from Alhaurín de la Torre, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes towards the coast, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Alhaurín De La Torre is defined by mountainous terrain, fertile valleys, and well-maintained roads with challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to advanced ascents. The routes in Alhaurín De La Torre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 36,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhaurín De La Torre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: The region offers a diverse terrain, situated between the eastern slopes of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas mountain range&lt;/strong&gt; and the fertile &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from pine forests and lush vegetable gardens to challenging mountainous sections like &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Jabalcuza&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Jarapalos&lt;/strong&gt;. The roads are generally well-maintained, providing a mix of gentle rides and rigorous ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: Yes, Alhaurín de la Torre offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. There are &lt;strong&gt;55 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; available. A great option for a gentle ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938984108/alhaurin-de-la-torre-circular-route-to-alhaurin-el-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhaurín de la Torre – circular route to Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.1 miles (27.6 km) long and takes about 1 hour 14 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some challenging climbs for advanced road cyclists in the area?
A: Alhaurín de la Torre is known for its challenging climbs. The region features significant ascents such as "The Antennas Climb," a 5 km stretch with a 10% grade. Other routes involve climbing towards "The Pescadores" in Pinos de Alhaurín and Alhaurín el Grande. Overall, there are &lt;strong&gt;60 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced cyclists seeking a rigorous workout, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10305008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coín – Alozaina Arch loop from Campanillas&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you can enjoy views of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas mountain range&lt;/strong&gt; and the fertile &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt;, often called the "Malaga vegetable patch," with its fruit trees and subtropical crops. Notable natural areas include &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Jabalcuza&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Jarapalos&lt;/strong&gt;. You might also encounter the remains of the 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7268549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de Zapata Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7268549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Santo Cristo del Cardón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves accessible near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Alhaurín de la Torre features several natural attractions. You can find the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4498964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Blanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular highlight. Additionally, there are several caves to explore, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6109990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Bull&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2600590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Trinidad Mine&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique geological insights.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: Alhaurín de la Torre benefits from a climate conducive to outdoor activities throughout much of the year. The region is particularly pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Even in winter, the climate is often favorable, making it an excellent destination for year-round cycling, though summer can be quite warm.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Alhaurín de la Torre are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938984108/alhaurin-de-la-torre-circular-route-to-alhaurin-el-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhaurín de la Torre – circular route to Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular option for its scenic loop.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and duration to accommodate all levels. You can find shorter, easy rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938984108/alhaurin-de-la-torre-circular-route-to-alhaurin-el-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhaurín de la Torre – circular route to Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.1 miles (27.6 km) and takes about 1 hour 14 minutes. For more challenging and longer rides, options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance bridge to El Burgo – View of Canillas de Aceituno loop from Campanillas&lt;/a&gt; can extend to over 90 miles (146 km) and take several hours.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: The road cycling routes around Alhaurín de la Torre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.4 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 600 reviews. Cyclists often praise the "perfect roads" and "very little traffic inland," contributing to an enjoyable and safe riding experience. The variety of terrain and scenic views are also frequently highlighted.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in total around Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: There are over &lt;strong&gt;180 road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Alhaurín de la Torre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This extensive network ensures that every cyclist can find a route that suits their needs, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs.

Q: Are there routes that lead to the coast from Alhaurín de la Torre?
A: Yes, you can find road cycling routes that take you towards the coast, offering varied landscapes and sea views. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Fuengirola – Elephant Roundabout loop from Alhaurín de la Torre&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail that leads through diverse scenery towards the beautiful Costa del Sol.

Q: What are some other outdoor activities or attractions in Alhaurín de la Torre besides cycling?
A: Beyond cycling, Alhaurín de la Torre offers several outdoor attractions. You can explore the &lt;strong&gt;City Park&lt;/strong&gt; with its botanical specimens, or visit the &lt;strong&gt;Jarapalos Arboretum&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its ecological value and hiking opportunities. The &lt;strong&gt;Jardín Oriental Bienquerido&lt;/strong&gt; provides a tranquil setting, and the area also features various caves and gorges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Adelfa Ravine&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Full of holes, horrible for road bike (nov 2025)

A city with a village soul. The most characteristic point is the church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación, which is located on the hill where there used to be the old castle and the Muslim Alhaurín mosque (in Andalusian times).
